intro: Glomera kerewensis, also known as the Kereuwera orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Glomera kerewensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antifungal, and antitumor activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the dried roots and rhizomes being used to make a tea or tincture.
